name: El Bosque de Colón to Alto de Castrove — circular
name_original: El Bosque de Colón a Alto de Castrove — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Poio offers diverse landscapes and natural features, making it suitable for various fitness levels. The region features a mix of coastal paths, forested areas, and routes that incorporate traditional architecture and historic sites. Trails range from short, easy walks to more demanding treks with significant elevation gains, providing varied terrain for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Poio The most popular hiking route is El Bosque de Colón to Alto de Castrove — circular, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through forested areas and offers potential panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruins of Santo Domingo Convent – Historic streets of Pontevedra loop from Poio, a difficult 12.1 miles (19.5 km) path. This trail combines urban exploration of historic streets with visits to significant cultural landmarks. Local hikers also love the Hórreos in Combarro – Armenteira Monastery loop from Bosque de Colón, a 14.8 miles (23.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas and towards a historic monastery, often completed in about 7 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Poio is defined by coastal paths, traditional hórreos, and historic monasteries. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Poio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Poio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Poio?
A: Poio offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking routes. These trails cater to various preferences, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance treks, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Poio?
A: Yes, Poio has 17 easy routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14934832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Padrón Beach – Hórreos in Combarro loop from Poio&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 km long and takes just over an hour, offering coastal views and a glimpse of traditional hórreos.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Poio?
A: Hiking in Poio reveals a rich tapestry of landscapes, from scenic coastal paths and traditional hórreos in villages like Combarro to lush forested areas and routes leading to historic monasteries. You'll find diverse terrain, including riverine landscapes and elevated viewpoints offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Poio?
A: Yes, many routes in Poio are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375590788/el-bosque-de-colon-to-alto-de-castrove-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bosque de Colón to Alto de Castrove — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 10.3 km through forested areas with potential panoramic views.

Q: What are some notable cultural or historical sights along the hiking trails in Poio?
A: Poio's trails often blend natural beauty with cultural heritage. You can explore traditional Galician hórreos, such as those in Combarro, or visit significant religious sites like the Armenteira Monastery. Some routes also pass through historic urban centers like Pontevedra, offering a glimpse into the region's past. Consider the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Santo Domingo Convent – Historic streets of Pontevedra loop from Poio&lt;/a&gt; for a mix of urban exploration and cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of lakes or rivers?
A: Yes, the region features several natural water bodies. While hiking, you might encounter highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1400940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Cotorredondo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/738182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontillón do Castro Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40333667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreos in Combarro – Armenteira Monastery loop from Bosque de Colón&lt;/a&gt;, traverse riverine landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Poio?
A: The hiking trails in Poio are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of natural beauty with cultural sites, and the variety of options for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers in Poio?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Poio offers 2 difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40333667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hórreos in Combarro – Armenteira Monastery loop from Bosque de Colón&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 23.8 km trail with nearly 700 meters of elevation gain, often taking over 7 hours to complete.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can discover several points of interest. These include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/450468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the traditional &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4379156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galician Hórreo&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3969392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Pilgrim Virgin&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also intersect with the historic Camino de Santiago, marked by milestones like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4411092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Km 60 marker&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Poio?
A: Poio offers enjoyable hiking experiences throughout much of the year. The varied terrain, from coastal paths to forested areas, means that different seasons can highlight different aspects of its beauty. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for longer treks, while summer is ideal for coastal walks.

Q: Are there any trails that combine urban exploration with natural scenery?
A: Yes, some routes in Poio offer a unique blend of urban and natural environments. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Santo Domingo Convent – Historic streets of Pontevedra loop from Poio&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, allowing you to explore the historic streets of Pontevedra before venturing into more natural surroundings.
tips: A magnificent viewpoint, with views of the Pontevedra estuary, above Poio. Giant sequoias imported from California were planted. These beautiful conifers will still be here long after your visit.
One of the highest peaks on the Galician Atlantic coast with views of the Pontevedra estuary. A combination of gravel trails and singletrack trails lead to the 630 m peak.
